channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Writing Code to Watch the Watchers: Log Aggression and Digital Self-Defense
item.description: The modern internet isn’t run by omniscient gods. It’s run by overworked sysadmins, bloated enterprise suites, and automated scrapers written by junior devs who copied code from Stack Overflow....

item.title: Code or diffusion? A field guide to programmatic image generation
item.description: A diagram is not a picture. It is a data structure that happens to be visible. Treat it as pixels and you pay pixel prices. $0.02 to $0.20 per diffusion render. No source file. No source-level way...

item.title: MS Project Gantt Is Dead — What Now?
item.description: For years, “Gantt chart” and “Microsoft Project” were almost interchangeable. The workflow is simple: create tasks, connect dependencies, check the critical path, assign resources, and export the...

item.title: 🚀 How We Organized a Frontend Quiz That Attracted 1,600+ Registrations
item.description: Building software is exciting. But building a community around technology? That's even more rewarding. Recently, our team at Omnikon organized our very first large-scale community event—the...

item.title: SDR Is the New Wireshark: Sniffing the Sub-GHz Spectrum from Your Desk
item.description: The modern security engineer is effectively blind. We spend our lives staring into the comforting, structured matrix of the OSI model. We audit TLS 1.3 handshakes, we fine-tune eBPF probes, and we...

item.title: SpaceX wants to launch 100k more Starlink satellites – for 100x the bandwidth
item.description: Article URL: https://www.zdnet.com/home-and-office/networking/spacex-wants-to-launch-100000-more-starlink-satellites/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48863064 Points: 5 #...

item.title: 5 Signals Framework: How I Hit 100% Job Success on Upwork
item.description: The Upwork 5-Signal Framework I Used to Hit 100% Job Success After months of inconsistent results on Upwork — wasted connects, ghosted clients, and scope creep — I realized the problem wasn't my...

item.title: AGENTS.md is advisory. Here's what it takes to make it executable
item.description: The pain: your agent file is a polite suggestion AGENTS.md is a great convention: one predictable place where a coding agent learns your build commands, style, and boundaries. Over 60k open-source...

item.title: Your AGENTS.md Is Already Stale — And Your Agent Trusts It Completely
item.description: TL;DR — A hand-written AGENTS.md rots the day you write it. And unlike a stale README (which a human skims skeptically), a stale AGENTS.md is obeyed by your coding agent with full confidence:...

item.title: How to Get Startup Grants: A First-Time Founder's Guide
item.description: Free money for your startup exists. That's the good news. The catch is that startup grants are competitive, slow, and surrounded by more misinformation than almost any other funding topic....

item.title: Nvidia’s biggest RAM supplier just had a trillion-dollar debut on Wall Street
item.description: As the AI boom boosts demand for RAM, SK Hynix - one of the world's biggest suppliers of memory chips - launched on Wall Street Friday. The South Korean chipmaker opened at $170 per share and...

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys...

item.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
item.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

item.title: Spotify will let you fine-tune your weekly Release Radar playlist
item.description: Spotify is giving listeners control to fine-tune what gets surfaced for them in Release Radar - one of its most popular weekly playlists. The new options allow you to narrow the playlist to a...

item.title: Netflix is turning into YouTube
item.description: Netflix has shows and movies. And video games. And live sports. And podcasts. And also, apparently, YouTube videos? For a company that used to seem like the next big thing in TV, it all feels a...

item.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips...

item.title: Disney Plus is reportedly looking into a free streaming tier
item.description: Disney Plus is considering making some of its content free to watch, according to a report from Business Insider. A source tells the outlet that Adam Smith, Disney's chief product and technology...

item.title: Anker’s 3-in-1 Qi2.2 charging station is $95 off
item.description: If you’re looking to declutter your nightstand and quickly charge up to three of your most-used gadgets, Anker’s Prime Wireless Charging Station is an easy way to do both. The station has spots...

item.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
item.description: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the...

item.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
item.description: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it...

item.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
item.description: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft,...

item.title: I’m filling in at The Verge for 6 weeks. Ask me anything!
item.description: Hello my friends! My protocol pals! My computational compadres! I'm David, a technology reporter and cohost of the Waveform Podcast. I'll be hosting a subscriber-only "AMA" today at 11AM PT / 2PM...

item.title: Empathy for the optimizers
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
item.description: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter...

item.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
item.description: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is...

item.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
item.description: The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON. Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the...

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over...

item.title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description: A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch. FoxIO...

item.title: From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description: Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability...

item.title: Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists...
